text: IEEE Std 1547-2003 provides interconnection technical specifications and requirements.
IEEE Std 1547-2003 provides test specifications and requirements.
IEEE Std 1547-2003 includes a bibliography as Annex A that lists citations referred to in this standard for informative purposes.
The citations in Annex A are not required to be used in conjunction with IEEE Std 1547-2003.
IEEE Std 1547-2003 establishes criteria and requirements for interconnection of distributed resources (DR) with electric power systems (EPS).
IEEE Std 1547-2003 provides a uniform standard for interconnection of distributed resources with electric power systems.
IEEE Std 1547-2003 provides requirements relevant to the performance, operation, testing, safety considerations, and maintenance of the interconnection.
The requirements of IEEE Std 1547-2003 shall be met at the point of common coupling (PCC).
The devices used to meet the requirements of IEEE Std 1547-2003 can be located elsewhere than the point of common coupling.
IEEE Std 1547-2003 applies to interconnection based on the aggregate rating of all the DR units that are within the Local EPS.
The functions of the interconnection system hardware and software that affect the Area EPS are required to meet IEEE Std 1547-2003 regardless of the location of these functions on the EPS.
The stated specifications and requirements in IEEE Std 1547-2003, both technical and testing, are universally needed for interconnection of DR.
The stated specifications and requirements in IEEE Std 1547-2003 include synchronous machines, induction machines, or power inverters/converters.
The stated specifications and requirements in IEEE Std 1547-2003 will be sufficient for most installations.
The criteria and requirements in IEEE Std 1547-2003 are applicable to all distributed resource technologies with aggregate capacity of 10 MVA or less at the PCC.
The criteria and requirements in IEEE Std 1547-2003 apply to distributed resources interconnected to EPSs at typical primary and/or secondary distribution voltages.
Installation of DR on radial primary and secondary distribution systems is the main emphasis of IEEE Std 1547-2003.
Installation of DR on primary and secondary network distribution systems is considered in IEEE Std 1547-2003.
IEEE Std 1547-2003 is written considering that the DR is a 60 Hz source.
IEEE Std 1547-2003 does not define the maximum DR capacity for a particular installation that may be interconnected to a single PCC.
IEEE Std 1547-2003 does not define the maximum DR capacity connected to a given feeder.
IEEE Std 1547-2003 does not prescribe DR self-protection.
IEEE Std 1547-2003 does not prescribe all operating requirements for DR units.
IEEE Std 1547-2003 does not address planning, designing, operating, or maintaining the Area EPS.

IEEE Std 1547-2003 does not apply to automatic transfer schemes in which load is transferred between the DR and the EPS in a momentary make-before-break operation provided the duration of paralleling the sources is less than 100 ms, except as noted in section 4.1.4.
The following standards shall be used in conjunction with IEEE Std 1547-2003.
When the stated version of the referenced standards is superseded by an approved revision, then that revision shall apply.
The applicability of the referenced standards is determined by the specific requirements stated in IEEE Std 1547-2003.
ANSI C84.1-1995 is titled Electric Power Systems and Equipment—Voltage Ratings (60 Hz).
IEEE Std C37.90.1-2002 is titled IEEE Standard Surge Withstand Capability (SWC) Tests for Relays and Relay Systems Associated with Electric Power Apparatus.
IEEE Std C37.90.2-1995 is titled IEEE Standard Withstand Capability of Relay Systems to Radiated Electromagnetic Interference from Transceivers.
IEEE Std C62.41.2-2002 is titled IEEE Recommended Practice on Characterization of Surges in Low Voltage (1000 V and less) AC Power Circuits.
IEEE Std C62.45-2002 is titled IEEE Recommended Practice on Surge Testing for Equipment Connected to Low-Voltage (1000V and Less) AC Power Circuits.
NEMA MG 1-1998 is titled Motors and Generators, Revision 2.
IEEE 100, The Authoritative Dictionary of IEEE Standards Terms, Seventh Edition, should be referenced for terms not defined in IEEE Std 1547-2003.
Area electric power system operator (Area EPS Operator) is the entity responsible for designing, building, operating, and maintaining the Area EPS.
Cease to energize means cessation of energy outflow capability.
Design test is a test of one or more devices made to a certain design to show that the design meets certain specifications.
Distributed generation (DG) consists of electric generation facilities connected to an Area EPS through a PCC.
Distributed generation is a subset of distributed resources.
Distributed resources (DR) are sources of electric power that are not directly connected to a bulk power transmission system.
Distributed resources include both generators and energy storage technologies.
Electric power system (EPS) consists of facilities that deliver electric power to a load.
Area electric power system (Area EPS) is an EPS that serves Local EPSs.
Local electric power system (Local EPS) is an EPS contained entirely within a single premises or group of premises.
Interconnection is the result of the process of adding a DR unit to an Area EPS.
Interconnection equipment consists of individual or multiple devices used in an interconnection system.
Interconnection system is the collection of all interconnection equipment and functions, taken as a group, used to interconnect a DR unit(s) to an Area EPS.
Inverter is a machine, device, or system that changes direct-current power to alternating-current power.

Island is a condition in which a portion of an Area EPS is energized solely by one or more Local EPSs through the associated PCCs while that portion of the Area EPS is electrically separated from the rest of the Area EPS.
Intentional island is a planned island.
Unintentional island is an unplanned island.
Non-islanding is intended to prevent the continued existence of an island.
Point of common coupling (PCC) is the point where a Local EPS is connected to an Area EPS.
Point of distributed resources connection (point of DR connection) is the point where a DR unit is electrically connected in an EPS.
Simulated utility is an assembly of variable frequency and variable voltage test equipment used to simulate a normal utility source.
Total demand distortion (TDD) is the total root-sum-square harmonic current distortion, in percent of the maximum demand load current (15 or 30 minute demand).

Total rated-current distortion (TRD) is the total root-sum-square of the current harmonics created by the DR unit operating into a linear balanced load divided by the greater of the test load current demand (IL) or the rated current capacity of the DR unit (Irated).
Area EPS stands for area electric power system.
DG stands for distributed generation.
DR stands for distributed resources.
EMI stands for electromagnetic interference.
EPS stands for electric power system.
Local EPS stands for local electric power system.
PCC stands for point of common coupling.
SCC21 stands for Standards Coordinating Committee 21.
SWC stands for surge withstand capability.
TDD stands for total demand distortion.
TRD stands for total rated-current distortion.

For an unintentional island in which the DR energizes a portion of the Area EPS through the PCC, the DR interconnection system shall detect the island and cease to energize the Area EPS within two seconds of the formation of an island.
Intentional islanding is under consideration for future revisions of IEEE Std 1547-2003.
Clause 5 of IEEE Std 1547-2003 provides the test requirements to demonstrate that the interconnection system meets the requirements of Clause 4.
The applicable tests from Clause 5 are required for all interconnection systems.
The results of the tests in Clause 5 shall be formally documented.
The design test applies to a packaged interconnection system using embedded components.
The design test applies to an interconnection system that uses an assembly of discrete components.
The design test shall be conducted on the same sample in the sequence of Table 4.
The design test shall be performed as applicable to the specific interconnection system technology.
The design test shall be performed on a representative sample, either in the factory, at a testing laboratory, or on equipment in the field.
The design test applies to a packaged interconnection system using embedded components.
The design test applies to an interconnection system that uses an assembly of discrete components.

The response to abnormal voltage and frequency test shall demonstrate that the DR ceases to energize the Area EPS when the voltage or frequency exceeds the limits as specified in sections 4.2.3 and 4.2.4.

metadata.clause_title: Response to Abnormal Voltage and Frequency

text: Interconnection systems provided with field adjustable set points shall also be tested at the minimum, midpoint, and maximum of the adjustable set point ranges.

metadata.clause_title: Response to Abnormal Voltage and Frequency

text: The response to abnormal voltage and frequency tests shall be conducted using either the simulated utility or secondary injection method.

metadata.clause_title: Response to Abnormal Voltage and Frequency

text: Test results conforming to requirements of A, B, or C in section 5.1.2 are accepted as indicating compliance with the requirements of section 4.1.3.
The appropriate conditions to be met for specific interconnection system technology are specified in section 5.1.2.

For synchronous interconnection to an EPS, or an energized local EPS to an energized Area EPS, the synchronization test shall demonstrate that at the moment of the paralleling-device closure, all three parameters in Table 5 are within the stated ranges.

For synchronous interconnection to an EPS, or an energized local EPS to an energized Area EPS, the synchronization test shall also demonstrate that if any of the parameters are outside of the ranges stated in Table 5, the paralleling-device shall not close.
For induction interconnection, the induction machine shall be tested to determine the maximum start-up in-rush current.
The results of the induction machine test shall be used to estimate the starting voltage magnitude change.
The results of the induction machine test shall verify that the induction machine unit shall meet the synchronization requirements in section 4.1.3 and the flicker requirements in section 4.3.2.

An inverter-based interconnection system that produces fundamental voltage before the paralleling device is closed shall be tested according to the procedure for synchronous interconnection as stated in A of section 5.1.2.
All other inverter-based interconnection systems shall be tested to determine the maximum start-up current.
The results of the inverter-based interconnection system test shall be used to estimate the starting voltage magnitude change.

The results of the inverter-based interconnection system test shall verify that the inverter-based interconnection unit shall meet the synchronization requirements in section 4.1.3 and the flicker requirements in section 4.3.2.
The interconnection system shall be tested in accordance with IEEE Std C37.90.2-1995 to confirm that the results are in compliance with section 4.1.8.1 for protection from EMI.
The influence of EMI shall not result in a change in state or mis-operation of the interconnection system.

The interconnection system shall be tested for the requirement in section 4.1.8.2 in all normal operating modes in accordance with IEEE Std C62.45-2002 for equipment rated less than 1000 V to confirm that the surge withstand capability is met by using the selected test level(s) from IEEE Std C62.41.2-2002.
Interconnection system equipment rated greater than 1000 V shall be tested in accordance with manufacturer or system integrator designated applicable standards for surge withstand performance.
For interconnection system equipment signal and control circuits, surge withstand performance testing shall use IEEE Std C37.90.1-2002.
The results of surge withstand performance tests shall indicate the interconnection unit did not fail, did not misoperate, and did not provide misinformation.
A dielectric test across the open-circuited paralleling device shall be conducted to confirm compliance with the requirements of section 4.1.8.3.
A test or field verification shall be conducted to confirm that section 4.4.1 is met regardless of the selected method of detecting isolation.
Inverter based DR shall be tested to confirm that the DR does not inject dc current greater than prescribed limits that are listed in section 4.3.1.
The intent of the harmonics test is to assess that the DR unit meets the harmonic limits under a controlled set of conditions specified in section 4.3.3.

The DR shall be operated in parallel with a predominantly inductive voltage source with a short circuit current capacity ISC of not less than 20 times the DR rated output current at fundamental frequency.
The voltage and frequency output of the voltage source shall correspond to the rated voltage and frequency of the DR.
The unloaded voltage waveform produced by the Area EPS or simulated utility voltage source shall have a total harmonic distortion (THD) less than 2.5%.
The DR shall be operated at an output test load current, IL, of 33%, 66%, and at a level as close to 100% of rated output current as practical.
Total rated-current distortion (TRD) shall be used in place of TDD in the harmonics test.

TRD is the total rms value of the sum of the current harmonics created by the DR unit operating into a linear balanced load divided by the greater of the test load current (IL) demand or the rated current capacity of the DR unit (Irated).
The individual harmonic distortion and TRD of the DR output current shall be measured for the first 40 harmonics.
The harmonic current injections shall be exclusive of any harmonic currents due to harmonic voltage distortion present in the Area EPS without the DR connected.
The harmonics test results shall not exceed the values in section 4.3.3, Table 3.

As an alternative, a synchronous generator DR shall be tested to meet the requirements of section 4.3.3 either after installation or while powering a balanced resistive load and isolated from any other sources.
The voltage harmonics of a synchronous generator DR while powering a resistive load at 100% of the machine kVA rating shall not exceed the levels in Table 6.
Voltage harmonics shall be measured line to line for 3-phase/3 wire systems.
Voltage harmonics shall be measured line to neutral for 3-phase/4-wire systems.
Each interconnection system shall be subjected to the requirements of sections 5.1.1 and 5.1.2.
Interconnection systems with adjustable set points shall be tested at a single set of set points as specified by the manufacturer.
The production test may be conducted as a factory test or may be performed as part of a commissioning test (see section 5.4).
A system design verification shall be made to ensure that the requirements of section 4.1.2 for grounding integration with Area EPS have been met.
A system design verification shall be made to ensure that the requirements of section 4.1.7 for isolation device have been met.
A system design verification shall be made to ensure that the provisions for monitoring are in accordance with section 4.1.6.
A system design verification shall be made to ensure that the requirements of section 4.2.1 for Area EPS faults have been met.
A system design verification shall be made to verify the interconnection system is coordinated with the Area EPS reclosing practices in accordance with section 4.2.2.
All commissioning tests shall be performed based on written test procedures.
A visual inspection shall be made to ensure that the grounding coordination requirement of section 4.1.2 has been implemented.
A visual inspection shall be made to confirm the presence of the isolation device if required by section 4.1.7.
Initial commissioning tests shall be performed on the installed DR and interconnection system equipment prior to the initial parallel operation of the DR.
An operability test on the isolation device is required for commissioning.
Unintentional-islanding functionality testing as specified in section 5.4.1 is required for commissioning.
Cease to energize functionality testing as specified in section 5.4.2 is required for commissioning.
Any tests of section 5.1 that have not been previously performed on a representative sample and formally documented are required for commissioning.
Any tests of section 5.2 that have not been previously performed are required for commissioning.
The applicable tests of section 5.1 shall be repeated when functional software or firmware changes have been made on the interconnection system.

The applicable tests of section 5.1 shall be repeated when any hardware component of the interconnection system has been modified in the field, or replaced or repaired with parts different from the tested configuration.
Subsections 5.4.1 and 5.4.2, and the applicable tests of section 5.2 shall be repeated if protection settings have been changed after factory testing.
Subsections 5.4.1 and 5.4.2, and the applicable tests of section 5.2 shall be repeated if protection functions have been adjusted after the initial commissioning process.

A reverse-power or minimum power function, if used to meet the requirements of section 4.4.1, shall be tested using injection techniques or by adjusting the DR output and local loads to verify that the reverse power or minimum power function is met.
For non-islanding interconnection systems, section 5.4.2 satisfies the non-islanding functionality test requirement.

If tests in sections 5.4.1.1 and 5.4.1.2 are not applicable to the interconnection system, the interconnection system shall be tested in accordance with procedures provided by the manufacturer or system integrator.

The cease to energize functionality test shall check the cease to energize functionality by operating a load interrupting device and verifying the equipment ceases to energize its output terminals and does not restart/reconnect for the required time delay.
The cease to energize functionality test shall be performed on each phase individually.
The cease to energize functionality test verifies conformance to the cease to energize requirement of sections 4.1.4, 4.2.1, 4.2.2, 4.2.3, 4.2.4, and 4.4.1.

All interconnection-related protective functions and associated batteries shall be periodically tested at intervals specified by the manufacturer, system integrator, or the authority who has jurisdiction over the DR interconnection.
Periodic test reports or a log for inspection shall be maintained for interconnection-related protective functions and associated batteries.
3.1.16 total demand distortion (TDD): The total root-sum-square harmonic current distortion, in percent of the maximum demand load current (15 or 30 minute demand).

text: 4. Interconnection technical specifications and requirements The requirements in this clause shall be met at the PCC, although the devices used to meet these requirements can be located elsewhere. The requirements apply to interconnection of either a single DR unit based on that unit’s rating or multiple DR units within a single Local EPS, based on the aggregate rating of all the DR units that are within the Local EPS.

metadata.clause_title: Acronyms Area EPS Area electric power system DG distributed generation DR distributed resources EPS electric power system I current IL load current ISC short circuit current Local EPS Local electric power system PCC point of common coupling TDD total demand distortion TRD total rated-current distortion

text: The DR shall be operated in parallel with a predominantly inductive voltage source with a short circuit cur rent capacity ISC of not less than 20 times the DR rated output current at fundamental frequency. The voltage and frequency output of the voltage source shall correspond to the rated voltage and frequency of the DR. The unloaded voltage waveform produced by the Area EPS or simulated utility voltage source shall have a total harmonic distortion (THD) less than 2.5%.

metadata.clause_title: Paralleling device A dielectric test across the open-circuited paralleling device shall be conducted to confirm compliance with the requirements of 4.1.8.3.

text: 5. Interconnection test specifications and requirements This clause provides the test requirements to demonstrate that the interconnection system meets the require ments of Clause 4. The applicable tests from this clause are required for all interconnection systems. The results of these tests shall be formally documented.

metadata.clause_title: Intentional islanding This topic is under consideration for future revisions of this standard.

text: Individual harmonic order h < 11

metadata.clause_title: Production tests Each interconnection system shall be subjected to requirements of 5.1.1 and 5.1.2. Interconnection systems with adjustable set points shall be tested at a single set of set points as specified by the manufacturer. This test may be conducted as a factory test or may be performed as part of a commissioning test (see 5.4).

text: 3.1.2 cease to energize: Cessation of energy outflow capability.

text: 5.3.2 Isolation device A system design verification shall be made to ensure that the requirements of 4.1.7 have been met.

metadata.clause_title: Isolation device A system design verification shall be made to ensure that the requirements of 4.1.7 have been met.

text: 5.3.3 Monitoring provisions A system design verification shall be made to ensure that the provisions for monitoring are in accordance with 4.1.6.

metadata.clause_title: Monitoring provisions A system design verification shall be made to ensure that the provisions for monitoring are in accordance with 4.1.6.

text: 3.1.6.1 electric power system, area (Area EPS): An EPS that serves Local EPSs. NOTE—Typically, an Area EPS has primary access to public rights-of-way, priority crossing of property boundaries, etc., and is subject to regulatory oversight. See Figure 1.
